title: Delayed Threat Processing in EU Region

communications.message: We are investigating reports of delayed threat processing for customers in the EU region. Threats are still being detected and queued for analysis, but processing is taking longer than normal. Threat detection in US and Asia-Pacific regions is operating normally. Our team is actively investigating the cause and we will provide an update within 30 minutes.

We have identified a database infrastructure issue affecting threat processing speed in the EU region. Our team has initiated failover procedures to restore normal processing times. All threats continue to be detected and queued—no threats are being missed and no data has been lost. Threat detection in US and Asia-Pacific regions remains unaffected. We will provide another update within 30 minutes.

Threat processing speeds in the EU region are returning to normal following infrastructure adjustments. We are monitoring the recovery closely to ensure stability. Processing delays have decreased significantly and we expect full resolution shortly. We will confirm resolution once all metrics have returned to baseline.

This incident has been resolved. Threat processing in the EU region has returned to normal speed. All queued threats have been processed and the system is operating as expected.

Incident Summary:
- Start time: Approximately 7:25 AM PT on March 20, 2025
- Resolution time: 9:45 AM PT on March 20, 2025
- Duration: Approximately 2 hours 20 minutes
- Impact: Customers in the EU region experienced delayed threat processing. All threats were detected and queued, and no threats were missed or data lost.

We apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused.
